messages.content: tell me an authentic mongolian recipe that has at least 25 grams of protein per serving

Mongolian Beef Stir-Fry

Ingredients:
- 1 lb beef sirloin, sliced thinly
- 1 tbsp vegetable oil
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 onion, chopped
- 1 red bell pepper, sliced
- 1 green bell pepper, sliced
- 1 cup sliced mushrooms
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 2 tbsp oyster sauce
- 1 tbsp brown sugar
- 1 tbsp cornstarch
- 1 tsp black pepper
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/2 cup water
- 2 green onions, sliced

Directions:

1. Mix together soy sauce, oyster sauce, brown sugar, cornstarch, black pepper, salt, and water in a bowl. Set aside.

2. Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add in garlic and onion, and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes.

3. Add in beef and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until browned.

4. Add in bell peppers and mushrooms and stir-fry for an additional 2-3 minutes.

5. Pour in the sauce mixture and stir-fry for a final 2-3 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats the beef and vegetables.

6. Garnish with green onions and serve hot with rice.

This dish contains approximately 35 grams of protein per serving.
tell me an authentic Xhosa recipe that has at least 25 grams of protein per serving

Xhosa Chicken Potjie

Ingredients:
- 1 whole chicken, cut into pieces
- 2 onions, chopped
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 red bell pepper, sliced
- 1 green bell pepper, sliced
- 2 carrots, sliced
- 2 potatoes, peeled and cubed
- 1 can (400g) chopped tomatoes
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 2 tsp ground coriander
- 2 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp ground turmeric
- 1 tsp paprika
- 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
- 1/2 tsp ground cardamom
- 2 cups chicken broth
- Salt and pepper
- Fresh parsley, for garnish

Directions:

1. Heat the oil in a large potjie pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the chicken pieces and brown on all sides. Remove the chicken from the pot, and set aside.

2. Add the onions and garlic to the pot and sauté until the onions are translucent.

3. Add the bell peppers, carrots, and potatoes to the pot, and sauté for 5-10 minutes or until the vegetables start to soften.

4. Add the canned tomatoes, chicken broth, and all of the spices to the pot.

5. Stir everything together, and add the chicken back to the pot.

6. Bring the pot to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and let the potjie simmer, stirring occasionally, for 1-2 hours or until the chicken is fully cooked and tender.

7. Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot with bread or rice.

This dish contains approximately 30 grams of protein per serving.
tell me an authentic mongolian recipe that has at least 25 grams of protein per serving, it shouldn't contain any chicken

Mongolian Beef Noodle Soup

Ingredients:
- 1 lb beef sirloin, thinly sliced
- 1 tbsp vegetable oil
- 1 onion, chopped
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tsp ginger, grated
- 1 red bell pepper, sliced
- 1 green bell pepper, sliced
- 4 cups beef broth
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp hoisin sauce
- 1 tbsp sugar
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 1 tbsp cornstarch
- 1 tbsp water
- 8 oz noodles (any kind)
- 2 green onions, sliced
- 1 tbsp sesame oil

Directions:

1. Heat vegetable oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add the beef and stir-fry until browned. Remove the beef from the pot and set aside.

2. Add onion, garlic and ginger to the same pot and sauté until fragrant.

3. Add the bell peppers and beef broth to the pot and bring to a boil.

4. Add the soy sauce, hoisin sauce, sugar, and black pepper and stir well.

5. Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes.

6. In a small bowl, stir together cornstarch and water. Add the mixture to the pot and stir.

7. Add the noodles to the pot and cook until tender, about 5-7 minutes.

8. Return the beef to the pot and simmer until heated through.

9. Add the green onions and sesame oil to the pot and stir.

10. Serve hot and enjoy your Mongolian Beef Noodle Soup!

This dish contains approximately 35-40 grams of protein per serving depending on the amount of beef used.

Xhosa Lamb Potjie

Ingredients:
- 1 kg lamb chops, cut into pieces
- 2 onions, chopped
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1 red bell pepper, sliced
- 1 green bell pepper, sliced
- 2 carrots, sliced
- 2 potatoes, peeled and cubed
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 2 tsp ground coriander
- 2 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp paprika
- 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
- 1/2 tsp ground cardamom
- 2 cups beef broth
- Salt and pepper
- Fresh parsley, for garnish

Directions:

1. Heat the oil in a large potjie pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the lamb chops pieces and brown on all sides. Remove the lamb from the pot, and set aside.

2. Add the onions and garlic to the pot and sauté until the onions are translucent.

3. Add the bell peppers, carrots, and potatoes to the pot, and sauté for 5-10 minutes or until the vegetables start to soften.

4. Add the chopped tomatoes, lamb broth, and all of the spices to the pot.

5. Stir everything together, and add the lamb back to the pot.

6. Bring the pot to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and let the potjie simmer, stirring occasionally, for 1-2 hours or until the lamb is fully cooked and tender.

7. Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot with mashed potatoes or bread.

This dish contains approximately 30-35 grams of protein per serving depending on the amount of lamb used.
tell me an authentic vietnamese recipe that has at least 25 grams of protein per serving, it shouldn't contain any chicken

Vietnamese Grilled Pork Chops (Sườn Nướng)

Ingredients:
- 1 lb pork chops (bone-in or boneless)
- 1 tbsp vegetable oil
- 1/4 cup lemongrass, minced
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 2 shallots, minced
- 2 tbsp honey
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 2 tbsp fish sauce
- 1 tbsp dark soy sauce
- 1/2 tsp black pepper
- 1/2 tsp sea salt
- Lime wedges, for serving

Directions:

1. In a medium bowl, mix together the vegetable oil, lemongrass, garlic, and shallots.

2. Add the honey, soy sauce, fish sauce, dark soy sauce, black pepper, and salt to the bowl and whisk until well combined.

3. Add the pork chops to the bowl and make sure that the meat is evenly coated in the marinade. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the pork marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, or overnight for maximum flavor.

4. Preheat your grill to medium-high heat. Remove the pork chops from the marinade, letting any excess marinade drip off, and discard the remaining marinade.

5. Grill the pork chops for 4-5 minutes on each side, or until cooked through.

6. Serve the pork chops with a side of vegetables or rice and lime wedges for squeezing over the top.

This dish contains approximately 25-30 grams of protein per serving depending on the size and cut of the pork chops used.
tell me an authentic Marathi recipe that has at least 25 grams of protein per serving, it shouldn't contain any chicken

Marathi-style Prawn Curry (Kolambi or Jhinga Curry)

Ingredients:
- 1 lb prawns, cleaned and deveined
- 2 onions, chopped
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 2 tsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tsp red chilli powder
- 2 tsp coriander powder
- 2 tsp cumin powder
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp garam masala
- Salt to taste
- 1/4 cup fresh cilantro leaves, chopped

Directions:

1. Heat vegetable oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add the onions and sauté until they turn translucent.

2. Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for a minute. Add tomatoes, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, and salt to the pan. Cook for 5-7 minutes or until the tomatoes are soft and mushy.

3. Add the prawns to the pan and mix well with the spices.

4. Cover the pan and cook for 5-7 minutes or until the prawns are fully cooked.

5. Remove the lid and add garam masala. Mix well and cook over low heat for 2-3 minutes.

6. Add chopped fresh cilantro leaves to the curry. Stir well and remove from heat.

7. Serve hot with rice or Indian bread.

This dish contains approximately 25-30 grams of protein per serving depending on the amount of prawns used.
tell me an authentic Goan Konkani recipe that has at least 25 grams of protein per serving, it shouldn't contain any chicken

Goan Konkani Fish Curry (Xit Codi)

Ingredients:
- 1 lb fish (preferably kingfish or pomfret)
- 1 onion, chopped
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 1 can (400ml) coconut milk
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 2 tsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tsp red chilli powder
- 2 tsp coriander powder
- 2 tsp cumin powder
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 1/2 tsp mustard seeds
- 1/2 tsp fenugreek seeds
- A pinch of asafetida (hing)
- Salt to taste
- 1/4 cup fresh cilantro leaves, chopped

Directions:

1. Heat vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add mustard seeds and fenugreek seeds to the pot. Saute until the mustard seeds start to pop.

2. Add the chopped onion to the pot. Saute until the onion is translucent.

3. Add ginger-garlic paste and saute for a minute.

4. Add tomatoes, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, and salt to the pot. Cook for 5-7 minutes or until the tomatoes are soft and mushy.

5. Add the fish to the pot and mix well with the spices.

6. Cover the pot and cook for 10-12 minutes or until the fish is fully cooked.

7. Add the can of coconut milk and mix well. Simmer the curry on low heat for 4-5 minutes.

8. Add chopped fresh cilantro leaves to the curry. Stir well and remove from heat.

9. Serve hot with rice or Indian bread.

This dish contains approximately 25-30 grams of protein per serving depending on the amount of fish used.
tell me an authentic Cebuano recipe that has at least 25 grams of protein per serving, it shouldn't contain any chicken

Cebuano Beef Steak (Bistek Cebu)

Ingredients:
- 1 lb beef sirloin, sliced thinly
- 1/4 cup soy sauce
- 1/4 cup calamansi juice or lemon juice
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 2 onions, sliced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 1/4 cup scallions, chopped
- 1/4 cup fresh cilantro leaves, chopped
- 1 red chili pepper, chopped (optional)

Directions:

1. In a mixing bowl, combine the soy sauce and calamansi juice or lemon juice.

2. Add the beef sirloin in the bowl and mix until the meat is evenly coated. Marinate for at least 30 minutes or overnight in the refrigerator for maximum flavor.

3. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat the vegetable oil. Add the sliced onions and garlic and sauté until the onions are caramelized and the garlic is fragrant.

4. Add the marinated beef to the skillet and cook over medium heat. Season the beef with salt and pepper to taste.

5. Cook the beef for 5-7 minutes or until the meat is browned on all sides and fully cooked.

6. Add chopped scallions, cilantro, and red chili pepper (if using) to the skillet and stir well.

7. Remove from heat and serve hot with rice.

This dish contains approximately 25-30 grams of protein per serving depending on the amount of beef used.
tell me an authentic Kinyarwanda recipe that has at least 25 grams of protein per serving, it shouldn't contain any chicken

Kinyarwanda Beef Stew (Isombe-ibinyomoro)

Ingredients:
- 2 lbs beef stew meat, cut into small pieces
- 2 onions, chopped
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 1 bell pepper, sliced
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tbsp fresh ginger, minced
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 2 tsp paprika
- 2 tsp cumin
- 2 tsp coriander
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 3 cups beef broth
- 1/2 lb sweet potatoes, peeled and cubed
- 1/2 lb eggplant, peeled and cubed
- 1/2 lb spinach, chopped
- 1/4 cup fresh cilantro leaves, chopped

Directions:

1. Heat vegetable oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onions, garlic, and ginger and sauté until the onions are translucent.

2. Add the beef stew meat to the pot and cook until browned on all sides.

3. Add paprika, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper to the pot and stir well.

4. Add chopped tomatoes and bell pepper to the pot and cook for 5-7 minutes or until the vegetables are soft.

5. Pour the beef broth into the pot and add sweet potatoes and eggplant. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 30-40 minutes or until the beef and vegetables are fully cooked.

6. Add chopped spinach to the pot and cook for 5-7 minutes or until the spinach has wilted.

7. Garnish with fresh cilantro leaves and serve hot with rice or bread.

This dish contains approximately 25-30 grams of protein per serving depending on the amount of beef used.
tell me an authentic Burmese recipe that has at least 25 grams of protein per serving, it shouldn’t contain any chicken

Burmese Pork Curry (Wet Tha Hin)

Ingredients:
- 1 lb pork belly, cut into small cubes
- 2 onions, chopped
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 inch ginger, minced
- 4 tomatoes, chopped
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- 2 tsp turmeric powder
- 2 tsp chili powder
- 1 tbsp fish sauce
- Salt to taste
- 2 cups water
- Fresh cilantro leaves, for garnish

Directions:

1. Heat vegetable oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onions, garlic, and ginger and sauté until the onions are translucent.

2. Add the pork belly to the pot and cook until browned on all sides.

3. Add turmeric powder, chili powder, fish sauce, and salt to the pot and stir well.

4. Add chopped tomatoes to the pot and cook for 5-7 minutes or until the tomatoes are soft.

5. Pour 2 cups of water into the pot and stir well. Bring the curry to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 30-40 minutes or until the pork is fully cooked and tender.

6. Remove the lid and let the curry simmer uncovered for another 10-15 minutes until the sauce thickens to your desired consistency.

7. Garnish with fresh cilantro leaves and serve hot with rice or bread.

This dish contains approximately 25-30 grams of protein per serving depending on the amount of pork used.
tell me an authentic Cebuano recipe that has at least 25 grams of protein per serving, it shouldn’t contain any chicken

Cebuano Grilled Tuna Belly (Inihaw na Lapu-lapu)

Ingredients:
- 2 lbs tuna belly, sliced
- 1/2 cup soy sauce
- 1/4 cup calamansi juice or lemon juice
- 1/4 cup brown sugar
- 2 tsp black pepper
- 1 head garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp vegetable oil
- Fresh cilantro leaves, for garnish

Directions:

1. In a mixing bowl, combine the soy sauce, calamansi juice or lemon juice, brown sugar, black pepper, and minced garlic.

2. Add the tuna belly slices in the bowl and mix until the fish is evenly coated in the marinade. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the tuna marinate in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes, or overnight for maximum flavor.

3. Preheat your grill to high heat. Remove the tuna slices from the marinade and discard the remaining marinade.

4. Brush vegetable oil onto the grill grates to prevent sticking. Place the tuna slices on the grill and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until the fish is cooked through.

5. Garnish with fresh cilantro leaves and serve hot with rice or salad.

This dish contains approximately 25-30 grams of protein per serving depending on the amount of tuna used.
